markdown_content:
## Executive Summary

Yannick requested testing of Google Gemini API and creation of demo scripts. Manus successfully tested the API, created comprehensive demo scripts, and produced detailed documentation including capabilities guide, quick reference, and README. The testing validated core features including text generation, streaming, structured outputs, and function calling, while hitting expected rate limits.


## Context & Intent

User needed practical understanding of Google Gemini API capabilities and working demo scripts for implementation and evaluation purposes


## What Was Done

Comprehensive API testing, demo script development, documentation creation covering 10 key features, 15 use cases, model comparison, and best practices


## Outputs Produced

- [script] gemini_api_demo.py — Complete Python demo script with 10 key Gemini API features
- [documentation] gemini_api_capabilities.md — In-depth guide covering all API capabilities and models
- [reference] gemini_quick_reference.md — 15 ready-to-use code snippets for common scenarios
- [guide] README.md — Getting started guide with troubleshooting and best practices

## Key Decisions & Validations

- Selected comprehensive testing approach covering core features
- Created production-ready code patterns
- Included rate limiting and error handling

## Lessons Learned

Worked well:

- API responded successfully to all test scenarios
- Structured output generation worked as expected
- Built-in tools provide significant value
Failed / suboptimal:

- Hit free tier rate limits during testing
- Some advanced features require paid tier
Discoveries:

- Gemini offers native multimodal capabilities
- Flash models provide excellent cost-performance ratio
- Built-in Google Search and Code Execution tools eliminate external dependencies

## Challenges & Blockers

- Rate limiting on free tier
- API key configuration requirements

## Open Questions

- What specific use cases will be prioritized for implementation?
- Should focus be on cost optimization or maximum capabilities?

## Next Steps

- Review demo scripts and documentation
- Set up API key and test locally
- Identify specific integration requirements
- Consider upgrade to paid tier for production use
